posts.title: Avenue of the Giants — Northern California Redwoods
posts.excerpt: Driving through towering redwoods along the Avenue of the Giants felt like entering another world.

body.content: We pulled off Highway 101 onto the Avenue of the Giants and immediately the world changed. Towering redwoods lined both sides of the road, their canopies filtering the sunlight into soft green beams.

body.alt: Towering redwoods along Avenue of the Giants

body.content: We stopped at the Founder's Grove trailhead and took the loop trail through some of the oldest and tallest trees in the park.
The kids were in awe — you really can't understand the scale of these trees until you're standing at the base of one looking straight up.

body.alt: Looking up at the redwood canopy

posts.title: Inner Space Cave Tour — Austin, Texas
posts.excerpt: A underground adventure through limestone formations right in the Texas Hill Country.

body.content: Inner Space Cavern is a karst cave discovered in 1963 during construction of Interstate 35. We took the Adventure Tour which goes through some of the most spectacular formations.
The stalactites and stalagmites were incredible. Our guide explained that some of these formations are over 100,000 years old.
The temperature stays around 72 degrees year-round, which was a welcome break from the Texas heat above ground.
The kids loved the section where the guide turned off all the lights to show true cave darkness — you literally couldn't see your hand an inch from your face.

body.alt: Family photo inside Inner Space Cavern
